mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in industrial production signals expanding real economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. The mechanism is always active; no threshold is required. The effect is confirmed when INDPRO shows a 6-month moving average above its 12-month moving average and subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 60% of the time.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO trend (6-month MA minus 12-month MA) for predicting 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 5-year window, or the hit rate in positive trend periods is not statistically above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate above 0.2 percentage points signals labor market improvement that raises consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is triggered only when the decline exceeds this threshold; otherwise no position is taken.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by more than 0.2 percentage points from previous month
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward S&P 500 return after a qualifying UNRATE decline is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion that boosts corporate profits and stock prices. The signal depends on the joint behavior of both series rising month-over-month; it is not decomposable into single-series effects. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and INDPRO show month-over-month increases in the latest release
failure_condition: When both series rise, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more often than positive (hit rate < 50%), or the incremental predictive power over single-series signals is zero in a nested model test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near the zero lower bound (below 0.25%), the usual easing signal is absent and further monetary accommodation must come from unconventional tools. In this regime, low rates are already priced in and any uptick in inflation expectations can trigger rate-hike fears, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns. The mechanism is regime-conditional: active only when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the average 5-day forward SPX return is positive, or the negative signal fails to predict returns below the unconditional mean (p > 0.10 in a one-sided test).

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real value of future corporate earnings and increases the likelihood of monetary tightening, both of which depress equity valuations. This always-on trend signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon when inflation momentum (3-month change) is positive.
failure_condition: The Spearman correlation between the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL and subsequent 5-day forward SPX returns is non-negative, or the conditional mean return following inflation acceleration is above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts (HOUST) above the 700,000 annualized units threshold signals a recovering housing sector, which stimulates construction activity, employment, and consumer wealth. This demand-side impulse leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month. The signal is triggered only when HOUST exceeds 700k in the latest release.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 700k, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate is below 55% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint improvement in the labor market (rising PAYEMS) and consumption (rising RSAFS) provides a strong signal of a self-reinforcing economic expansion. Employment gains boost income, which boosts spending, which in turn supports further hiring. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter horizon when both series are rising month-over-month.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS post month-over-month increases in the latest release
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return following joint increases is zero or negative, or the interaction term in a regression of returns on individual signals is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of low inflation and near-zero interest rates (CPIAUCSL year-over-year change < 3% and FEDFUNDS < 1%), any acceleration in inflation signals that the Fed may need to tighten policy sooner than expected, which is negative for equities over the intermediate term. This regime-conditional mechanism activates only when both conditions hold and predicts negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Within the regime, the correlation between CPIAUCSL month-over-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is positive, or the strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on trigger events yields a negative Sharpe ratio.
